source: Warm-up Essentials by Jimson Lee, IAAF New Studies in Athletics 29:1, 2014
principles: The warm-up IS part of the workout — not optional
Warmer ambient temperature = shorter warm-up needed
Static stretching before training can inhibit performance — use dynamic stretching instead
Mental preparation during warm-up is as important as physical

why_warmup_matters.muscle_pliability: Raises muscle length to optimal state for performance
why_warmup_matters.activation_mobilisation: Activates motor neurons and mobilises joints through full range of motion
why_warmup_matters.potentiation: Strengthens nerve impulses leading to faster, stronger muscle contraction
why_warmup_matters.oxygen_delivery: Increases blood flow to active muscles, delays fatigue
why_warmup_matters.injury_prevention: Warmer, looser muscle is less likely to cramp or pull under maximal effort
why_warmup_matters.mental_preparation: Focuses the athlete's mind before the session begins

warmup_phases.name: Raise Core Temperature
warmup_phases.description: Light jogging, skipping, shuffling. The goal is to warm the body — not to fatigue it.

exercises.name: Light jog
exercises.cue: Easy pace, land softly, arms relaxed

exercises.name: Side shuffle
exercises.cue: Both directions, stay light on feet

exercises.name: Skipping (forward)
exercises.cue: Lift knees, pump arms gently

exercises.name: Backward jog
exercises.cue: Look over shoulder, small steps

warmup_phases.name: Dynamic Flexibility
warmup_phases.description: Mobility work for all major joints. NOT static stretching — keep moving.

exercises.name: Leg swings (forward-back)
exercises.cue: Hold wall for balance, swing from hip — don't force range

exercises.name: Leg swings (side-to-side)
exercises.cue: Controlled, not ballistic — feel the hip open

exercises.name: Hip circles
exercises.cue: Both directions, hands on hips, big circles

exercises.name: Arm circles
exercises.cue: Both directions, start small and grow bigger

exercises.name: Torso rotations
exercises.cue: Feet planted, rotate from shoulders

exercises.name: Ankle rolls
exercises.cue: Both feet, both directions — prep the ankle joint

warmup_phases.name: Activation Drills (Gerard Mach Method)
warmup_phases.description: Sprint drills that activate the correct muscle patterns and nerve pathways. Used by elite track athletes globally. Do over 20-30m each.

exercises.name: A-Skip
exercises.cue: Drive knee up to hip height, foot dorsiflexed (toes up), push off ground with opposite foot. Rhythmic and controlled.

exercises.name: B-Skip
exercises.cue: A-skip with added knee extension — kick leg forward then pull back down. Slow and deliberate.

exercises.name: Butt Kicks (C-Drill)
exercises.cue: Heels kick up to glutes fast. Stay on toes. Short ground contact time.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.cue: Drive knees above hip height fast. Pump arms. Lean slightly forward.

exercises.name: Walking Lunges
exercises.cue: Step forward into deep lunge, back knee near ground. Keep chest tall. Alternate legs.

exercises.name: Lateral Shuffle
exercises.cue: Stay low, don't cross feet, push off outside foot. Good for football — covers defensive movement.

warmup_phases.name: Acceleration Strides
warmup_phases.description: Progressive runs to prime the nervous system for match/training speed. Full walk back between each.

exercises.name: Stride 1
exercises.cue: Start easy, build to half speed by 40m. Controlled.

exercises.name: Stride 2
exercises.cue: Build to 70% by end. Smooth, relaxed form.

exercises.name: Stride 3
exercises.cue: Near full pace by the end. Feel the power — don't force it.

warmup_phases.name: Potentiation (Optional — Competition Days)
warmup_phases.description: Explosive movements that prime the central nervous system for peak output. PAP effect lasts 2-30 minutes. Use before important training or matches.

exercises.name: Tuck jumps
exercises.cue: Jump and pull both knees to chest at peak. Land soft. Rest 30s between sets.

exercises.name: Vertical jump-ups
exercises.cue: Jump as high as possible, reach up. Absorb landing through knees.

exercises.name: Bounds (long jumps running)
exercises.cue: Cover maximum ground each stride. Drive off back foot hard.

notes: Football requires full lower body activation — do all 4 phases
Add ball work after Phase 3 if balls are available (passing circles, rondos at low intensity)
Goalkeepers: add shoulder rolls, wrist circles, and lateral dives at low intensity after Phase 2
Hot Zimbabwe weather: Phase 1 can be 3 minutes instead of 5
Cold morning sessions: extend Phase 1 to 7-8 minutes before moving to drills

common_mistakes: Static stretching before activity — saves this for AFTER training (cool-down)
Skipping the warm-up entirely when tired — this is when injury risk is highest
Warming up too intensely — you should feel warm and ready, not fatigued
Rushing through drills without correct technique — a sloppy A-skip teaches sloppy running patterns
Not matching warm-up to the session — a sprint session needs more activation than a technical ball session
cool_down_note: Static stretching belongs in the cool-down, not the warm-up. After training: hold each major muscle group 30-45 seconds. Focus on quads, hamstrings, hip flexors, calves, and lower back.
